events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: weep holes missing/blocked at bottom course. This is a flashing & weeps issue in masonry. Weep holes are required by TMS 402 and BIA tech notes to allow water that enters the cavity to drain out. Missing/blocked weep holes at the bottom course is non-compliant. The work is rough-complete. Decision should be fail. Risk: high (water intrusion, moisture damage). Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 85-90 since rough-complete. Value: 0 (no numeric reading asked). Confidence high. Findings: weep holes missing/blocked at bottom course preventing drainage of cavity. Actions: install/clear weep holes at proper spacing per code. References: TMS 402, BIA tech notes.

  "findings": [
    "Weep holes missing or blocked at the bottom masonry course, preventing drainage of the cavity/wall system.",
    "Flashing without functional weeps will trap water, risking moisture damage to framing, sheathing, and interior finishes."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Clear any blocked weep openings and install missing weeps at the bottom course at required spacing.",
    "Verify weep type and spacing comply with TMS 402 and BIA recommendations (e.g., 24 in. o.c. for rope/wick or 33 in. o.c. minimum for open weeps per applicable detail).",
    "Re-inspect after correction before closing out the wall."
  ],
  "rationale": "TMS 402 and BIA technical notes require functional weep holes above flashing at the bottom of the drainage cavity to allow water exit. Missing or blocked weeps at the bottom course are a non-compliant defect that compromises the flashing/weep system.",
  "references": [
    "TMS 402 - Masonry Standards Joint Committee Building Code Requirements for Masonry Structures",
    "BIA Technical Notes on Brick Construction - Flashing and Weep Holes"
  ]
}
